comments.author: @Kari.F.
comments.text:
It's a huge myth that Norway was dirt poor before we found oil. That wrongful assumption seems to be rooted in the undeniable fact that we don't have much fertile soil suitable for big agriculture compared to Germany; a country of the same size. 
But we had silver, and have always had a big fishing industry. Back in the olden days the whaling industry was among the largest in the world.

It's often pointed out that we were late to join the "party" of the Industrial Revolution, and therefore "backwards". What's never mentioned in that context is that when it DID come here, it grew so quickly that we surpassed many countries that started before us. That's because our nature made us uniquely blessed with practically limitless assess to hydro energy, while Britain and many countries in central Europe needed coal to run many of the machines of the new industries. Norway isn't all about the oil and natural gas today either, but that is what started the biggest sovereign Wealth Fund on the planet. A certain segments of the American population is often the quickest to pew-pew things they know absolutely nothing about, and Norway is no exception: If we withdrew all of our investments from the US tomorrow, the US would within months collapse into a mire of unemployment and bankruptcies that would be difficult to recover from. 
We have our issues like every other country on earth. But we are who we are today because generations of voters before us voted the way they did after WW2. After the Great Depression and the war, they wanted to build a country and an economy that would provide a better life for future generations of Norwegians than they had. For that I'm forever grateful!

comments.author: @Glenn-hm8sb
comments.text: This documentary is well made.., but.. As a Norwegian I know they are going too far. Look at our naighbors Sweeden and Denmark, Finland. This video is displaying us better than our sisters and brothers in our Nordic countries. Infact we are all in Scandinavia mixed with eachother and our boarders are only there by chance. Go visit Sweeden, Denmark and Finland. Countries that are better than us in Norway in many ways. I loved the scenary of my country here, but I think this attention we are getting has gone to far. Let us be honest. We have found a way to be economically independent, but our population sees nothing from our oil sirplus. And the Norwegian oilfund is used for future generations and also to help poor countries who needs it more than us. That is what I think makes us proud here. We are not capitalists here. This notion of us being filthy rich is not true. Sweeden is the imdustrial powerhouse in Scandinavia. They are way ahead than the rest of our Nordic countries and Finland is so underrated, that it is a crime. Happiest country to live in. In the world. Finland.

comments.author: @YonderNowNow
comments.text: The coolest thing about Norway isn't just the fjords; it's 'Allemannsretten'. It’s a law that gives everyone the 'Right to Roam.' You can legally pitch a tent almost anywhere in the wilderness for free, as long as you respect the land. Absolute paradise for explorers. 🏔⛺
